name: comment-checker
tagline: Tells Codex how to react to comment-checker warnings fired by a PostToolUse edit hook.
verdict: Clean and minimal (17 lines): no code, no network calls, no credential access, nothing hidden. It only describes plugin hook behavior and tells the agent to fix or explain a flagged comment. Minor documentation gaps only — no negative-trigger guidance, no defined output format, and no version/change signal — none of which pose a safety risk to a user who ins

highlights.summary: No explicit negative-trigger statement; only an indirect 'Non-edit tools are ignored by this plugin' hints at scope boundaries.
The only actionable step is one sentence ('fix or explain the flagged comment before moving on') with no concrete procedure for how to fix/explain or where to look.
No explicit output/response contract is defined for how Codex should format its fix-or-explain response.
